description:
You are the site administrator for a small service.  
    A configuration file in classic INI syntax already exists at  

        /home/user/admin_data/accounts.ini  

    Your goal is to generate a concise text report that lists every user account whose
    status is marked as “active” in that INI file.

    1. Read the file /home/user/admin_data/accounts.ini.  
       • Every user account is declared in its own section header, e.g.  
         [alice]  
         status   = active  
         role     = editor  

    2. Create a new directory  

        /home/user/admin_reports  

       if it does not yet exist.

    3. Inside that directory create a report file named  

        /home/user/admin_reports/active_users.log  

       The exact **mandatory** contents of active_users.log must follow
       this template **character-for-character** (automated tests will compare
       the file exactly):

       Active Users Report  
       -------------------  
       Total active users: <N>  

       List:  
       <user1>  
       <user2>  
       …  

       Rules for filling the template:  
       • Replace <N> with the exact count of active users.  
       • Replace <user1>, <user2>, … with the usernames copied
         verbatim from the section headers, preserving their original order of appearance
         in the INI file.  
       • There must be one blank line between the “Total active users” line and the “List:” line.  
       • Each user appears on its own line immediately after “List:”.  
       • No extra spaces must trail any line.  
       • The file ends with a single newline after the last username.

    4. Display the contents of /home/user/admin_reports/active_users.log to the terminal
       so the administrator can immediately see the generated summary.

    You should be able to accomplish everything with no more than 3–4 standard
    shell utilities (e.g., mkdir, grep, wc, awk, cut, printf, echo, cat).
truth:
Initial file that is already present:

    Path: /home/user/admin_data/accounts.ini
    Contents (exactly):

    [alice]
    status = active
    role   = editor

    [bob]
    status = disabled
    role   = admin

    [carol]
    status = active
    role   = viewer

    [dave]
    status = pending
    role   = contributor

    [eve]
    status = active
    role   = admin


    Expected artefact produced by the agent:

    Path: /home/user/admin_reports/active_users.log
    Final contents (exactly):

    Active Users Report
    -------------------
    Total active users: 3

    List:
    alice
    carol
    eve

    (note: the file ends with a single newline right after “eve”)
